signals.reasoning: BTC is stabilizing after the flush, but the board remains under full geopolitical veto and there is no clean fresh trigger. Staying flat is better than forcing a macro-sensitive entry here.
ETH has bounced with the rest of the market, but not in a way that resolves the bigger event risk. Under full veto, this remains a watch-only major rather than an actionable setup.
SOL has some squeeze fuel and a decent rebound, but the move is still just a recovery inside damaged structure. The active veto keeps this below the bar for a fresh long.
0G remains one of the strongest raw squeeze names on paper, but it is still extended, thin, and highly reversal-prone. Under a full veto, that is not a responsible chase.
STABLE still has very strong negative-funding squeeze mechanics, but the move is already late and heavily exposed to headline whipsaw. It stays watch-only instead of being upgraded under veto.
GOAT is already too deep into flush territory for a fresh short and too unstable for a fresh long. There is no high-quality new entry here.
AAVE has strong rebound structure and better liquidity than many alts on the board, but it is already extended enough that the reward-to-risk is poor under active geopolitical uncertainty.
BLUR has already done most of its downside work and now looks more like post-flush stabilization than a clean continuation short. That keeps it off the active list.
AVNT is weak overall but not offering a clean new entry. The chart is too damaged for a confident long and too mature for a clean fresh short.
GRASS has bounced sharply, but with only moderate liquidity and heavy event sensitivity it still does not clear the bar for a new long under veto.
MON still leans weak, but the current structure is more messy stabilization than clean breakdown continuation. It is better watched than traded here.

MANTA is one of the few names still showing active bearish structure, with price soft and open interest building on the short side. Confidence stays capped because of the veto and mediocre liquidity, but if anything on the board still deserves a short lean, this is one of the cleaner ones.
JTO has strong squeeze metrics, but the move is already underway and the board is fully vetoed again. That combination makes it a watchlist long, not an execution long.
TST lacks both liquidity quality and directional edge. There is no reason to force a position here.
BRETT looks like a short-covering bounce rather than a clean trend continuation in either direction. Under full veto, that is a pass.
GRIFFAIN remains weak, but most of the damage is already done and the current tape is not clean enough to trust as a fresh short. Best left alone.
BLAST has squeeze fuel but is still thin and messy, and the current event regime is hostile to chasing extended squeezes. It stays watch-only.
SKR is too thin and too neutral to justify a fresh trade. It does not have enough structure or liquidity to matter here.
FTT remains too illiquid and too spread-out to trust. Even if the chart leans weak, it is not a serious trade candidate.
REZ has squeeze fuel and some long-liquidation reset characteristics, but the setup is still too fragile and event-sensitive to upgrade under a full veto. It stays on the watchlist.
